figures.caption: Fig. 1: Comparison of MAPRPose with Baseline. (a) Baseline

Fig. 6: Convergence Analysis on LINEMOD. We compare the ADD-0.1d accuracy of our full model against the variant without amodal prediction across different refinement iterations (2, 4, 6, and 8). Our method achieves near-peak performance (99.8%) much faster than the baseline.

Fig. 2: Evaluations on BOP Benchmark. This benchmark evaluates 6D object pose estimation methods across BOP datasets. The Proposed approach achieves competitive accuracy and higher speed than prior methods.
TABLE I Comparison Results on LINEMOD. All results are reported under the same evaluation protocol; accuracy is measured by the ADD-0.1d metric (%).

Fig. 5: Qualitative Comparison on YCB-V Scenes. We compare various methods (FoundationPose, Co-op, FreeZe, MAPRPose (w/o AMPR) and MAPRPose) for estimating object poses across frames D, E, and F on YCB-V dataset. White boxes denote the ground truth poses, while colored boxes (blue, red, green, etc.) represent the estimated poses. MAPRPose (w/o AMPR) denotes our MAPRPose method without AMPR mechanism.

TABLE III Ablation Studies on MAPP Module. Accuracy is reported as ADD-0.1d (%) across LINEMOD objects. MAPP + FP refers to the configuration using MAPP for initial pose proposals followed by FoundationPose refinement. MAPRPose (w/o MAPP) represents our full framework without the MAPP module.
